title: Mega Gengar Battle Guide
summary: A top-tier [Ghost]/[Poison] [sweeper] whose [shadow-tag] traps opponents in place. 170 SpA, 130 Speed, and target-killing access make it one of the format most feared cleaners.
strengths: 170 SpA and 130 Speed is top-tier damage and Speed
weaknesses: 60/80/95 bulk is still fragile against priority

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternatives

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Gengar is a top-tier [Ghost]/[Poison] [sweeper] defined entirely by [shadow-tag]. The ability prevents opponents from switching, which turns Mega Gengar into a precision weapon that can eliminate specific team threats on demand. Combined with 170 SpA and 130 Speed, it is one of the most dangerous cleaners in the Champions format.

## Strengths
[shadow-tag] is the best offensive ability in the game — trapping an opponent means Mega Gengar can set up a [nasty-plot] turn and then delete the target with [shadow-ball] or [sludge-wave]. 170 SpA with [timid] nature produces top-tier damage, and Speed 130 outruns nearly every unboosted attacker. The [Ghost]/[Poison] STAB combination only misses on [Dark] and [Steel] pivots, which [focus-blast] handles directly.

## Weaknesses & Answers
60/80/95 bulk is still fragile — priority moves remain a concern and scarfed attackers above 130 can [revenge]-kill after setup. [Dark], [Ghost], [Ground], and [psychic] 2x weaknesses are unchanged, and [pursuit]-style trapping can flip the target hunting plan against Mega Gengar. The pre-Mega turn runs [cursed-body], which means the trap does not activate until evolution completes.

## Recommended Moveset
[shadow-ball] is the locked [Ghost] STAB for consistent damage. [sludge-wave] is the [Poison] STAB finisher that handles Fairies. [focus-blast] is the [Fighting] coverage slot for [Dark], [Steel], and [Normal] walls that resist both STABs. [nasty-plot] is the last slot and the setup win condition — one use is enough to guarantee a target KO.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[gengarite] is mandatory. [shadow-tag] activates post-evolution. Tera [Ghost] maxes [shadow-ball] damage for the cleanest trap-and-kill, while Tera [Fairy] flips [Dark] and [Dragon] weaknesses defensively during the setup turn. The defensive Tera is usually preferred because priority attackers are the biggest threat to the sweep.

## Teammates & Synergy
Mega Gengar wants [stealth-rock] or chip support that widens OHKO ranges after setup — every additional 12% damage makes traps more reliable. [Fairy] or [Steel] partners cover the [Dark] and [psychic] weaknesses, and a [taunt] lead shuts down opposing priority abusers. A cleaner behind Mega Gengar finishes weakened cores after the trap-and-kill turn resolves.

## Play Patterns
Mega Evolve into a predicted passive matchup, then [nasty-plot] on the trap turn and open with the STAB or coverage move that guarantees the KO. Save Tera for a telegraphed [Dark] or [psychic] move, and never commit to setup against a faster priority user without insurance.
